papers.title: Attention Is All You Need
papers.abstract: The dominant sequence transduction models are based on complex recurrent or convolutional neural networks that include an encoder and a decoder. The best performing models also connect the encoder and decoder through an attention mechanism. We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms, dispensing with recurrence and convolutions entirely.

papers.title: BERT: Pre-training of Deep Bidirectional Transformers for Language Understanding
papers.abstract: We introduce a new language representation model called BERT, which stands for Bidirectional Encoder Representations from Transformers. Unlike recent language representation models, BERT is designed to pre-train deep bidirectional representations from unlabeled text by jointly conditioning on both left and right context in all layers.

papers.title: Deep Residual Learning for Image Recognition
papers.abstract: Deeper neural networks are more difficult to train. We present a residual learning framework to ease the training of networks that are substantially deeper than those used previously. We explicitly reformulate the layers as learning residual functions with reference to the layer inputs, instead of learning unreferenced functions.

papers.title: Generative Adversarial Networks
papers.abstract: We propose a new framework for estimating generative models via an adversarial process, in which we simultaneously train two models: a generative model G that captures the data distribution, and a discriminative model D that estimates the probability that a sample came from the training data rather than G.

papers.title: Playing Atari with Deep Reinforcement Learning
papers.abstract: We present the first deep learning model to successfully learn control policies directly from high-dimensional sensory input using reinforcement learning. The model is a convolutional neural network trained with a variant of Q-learning, whose input is raw pixels and whose output is a value function estimating future rewards.

papers.title: ImageNet Large Scale Visual Recognition Challenge
papers.abstract: The ImageNet Large Scale Visual Recognition Challenge is a benchmark in object category classification and detection on hundreds of object categories and millions of images. The challenge has been running annually from 2010 to present, attracting participation from more than fifty institutions.

papers.title: Dropout: A Simple Way to Prevent Neural Networks from Overfitting
papers.abstract: We introduce dropout, a technique for reducing overfitting in neural networks by preventing complex co-adaptations on training data. The term dropout refers to dropping out units in a neural network. We show that dropout improves the performance of neural networks on supervised learning tasks in vision, speech recognition, document classification and computational biology.

papers.title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
papers.abstract: Recent work has demonstrated substantial gains on many NLP tasks and benchmarks by pre-training on a large corpus of text followed by fine-tuning on a specific task. GPT-3, our latest language model, has 175 billion parameters and shows strong performance on many NLP datasets with few-shot learning.

papers.title: An Image is Worth 16x16 Words: Transformers for Image Recognition at Scale
papers.abstract: While the Transformer architecture has become the de-facto standard for natural language processing tasks, its applications to computer vision remain limited. In vision, attention is either applied in conjunction with convolutional networks, or used to replace certain components of convolutional networks while keeping their overall structure in place.

papers.title: Mastering the Game of Go without Human Knowledge
papers.abstract: A long-standing goal of artificial intelligence is an algorithm that learns, tabula rasa, superhuman proficiency in challenging domains. AlphaGo Zero achieves superhuman performance in Go without using any human knowledge, demonstrating that reinforcement learning alone can achieve superhuman performance.

papers.title: Diffusion Models Beat GANs on Image Synthesis
papers.abstract: We show that diffusion models can achieve image sample quality superior to the current state-of-the-art generative models. We achieve this on unconditional image synthesis by finding a better architecture through a series of ablations. For conditional image synthesis, we further improve sample quality with classifier guidance.

papers.title: LLaMA: Open and Efficient Foundation Language Models
papers.abstract: We introduce LLaMA, a collection of foundation language models ranging from 7B to 65B parameters. We train our models on trillions of tokens, and show that it is possible to train state-of-the-art models using publicly available datasets exclusively.

papers.title: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models
papers.abstract: We present high quality image synthesis results using diffusion probabilistic models, a class of latent variable models inspired by considerations from nonequilibrium thermodynamics. Our best results are obtained by training on a weighted variational bound designed according to a novel connection between diffusion probabilistic models and denoising score matching with Langevin dynamics.

papers.title: CLIP: Learning Transferable Visual Models From Natural Language Supervision
papers.abstract: State-of-the-art computer vision systems are trained to predict a fixed set of predetermined object categories. We study the performance of CLIP, a model pre-trained to predict which caption goes with which image, and show it can be applied to most tasks without needing dataset-specific training.

papers.title: Neural Machine Translation by Jointly Learning to Align and Translate
papers.abstract: Neural machine translation is a recently proposed approach to machine translating directly using a large neural network. We conjecture that the use of a fixed-length vector is a bottleneck in improving the performance of this basic encoder-decoder architecture, and propose to extend this by allowing a model to automatically (soft-)search for parts of a source sentence.

papers.title: Safety and Efficacy of the BNT162b2 mRNA Covid-19 Vaccine
papers.abstract: A two-dose regimen of BNT162b2 (30 μg per dose, given 21 days apart) was found to be safe and 95% effective against Covid-19. The safety profile over a median of 2 months was characterized by short-term, mild-to-moderate pain at the injection site, fatigue, and headache.

papers.title: Global, Regional, and National Incidence, Prevalence, and Years Lived with Disability for 328 Diseases and Injuries
authors: GBD 2016 Disease and Injury Incidence Prevalence Collaborators
papers.abstract: The Global Burden of Disease Study 2016 provides comprehensive evidence on prevalence and years lived with disability for 328 causes in 195 countries and territories from 1990 to 2016. This analysis informs health policy decisions and research priorities.

papers.title: Hydroxychloroquine and Azithromycin as a Treatment of COVID-19
papers.abstract: We report the results of a clinical trial in 36 patients with COVID-19 treated with hydroxychloroquine, and its combination with azithromycin. Both hydroxychloroquine and azithromycin were found to be efficient in clearance of viral nasopharyngeal carriage of SARS-CoV-2.

papers.title: Remdesivir for the Treatment of Covid-19 - Final Report
papers.abstract: Remdesivir was superior to placebo in shortening the time to recovery in adults hospitalized with Covid-19 and evidence of lower respiratory tract infection. These data support remdesivir as a standard of care for hospitalized patients with Covid-19 who require supplemental oxygen therapy.

papers.title: Dexamethasone in Hospitalized Patients with Covid-19
papers.abstract: In patients hospitalized with Covid-19, the use of dexamethasone resulted in lower 28-day mortality among those who were receiving either invasive mechanical ventilation or oxygen alone at randomization, but not among those receiving no respiratory support.

papers.title: CRISPR-Cas9 for Medical Genetic Screens: Applications and Future Perspectives
papers.abstract: CRISPR-Cas9 has transformed the ability to manipulate gene expression and genome sequences in a precise, efficient and cost-effective manner. We review recent advances in CRISPR-based functional genomic screens and discuss the therapeutic implications.

papers.title: The Human Genome Project: Lessons from Large-Scale Biology
papers.abstract: The Human Genome Project has revealed that the human genome contains approximately 20,000 to 25,000 protein-coding genes. Here we review major lessons from this landmark project and discuss its impact on biology and medicine.

papers.title: Metformin and Cancer Risk and Mortality: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis Taking into Account Biases and Confounders
papers.abstract: We conducted a systematic review and meta-analysis of studies examining the association between metformin use and cancer incidence and mortality. Metformin was associated with a significant reduction in cancer incidence and mortality in observational studies.

papers.title: Artificial Intelligence in Radiology
papers.abstract: We review the current state of artificial intelligence applications in radiology, including image interpretation, workflow optimization, and clinical decision support. Deep learning models have demonstrated performance comparable to radiologists in specific tasks.

papers.title: Global Cancer Statistics 2020: GLOBOCAN Estimates of Incidence and Mortality Worldwide
papers.abstract: In 2020, there were 19.3 million cancer cases and almost 10 million cancer deaths globally. Female breast cancer has surpassed lung cancer as the most commonly diagnosed cancer. These estimates highlight the need for improved cancer prevention and control strategies.
papers.journal: CA: A Cancer Journal for Clinicians

papers.title: Observation of Gravitational Waves from a Binary Black Hole Merger
papers.abstract: On September 14, 2015 at 09:50:45 UTC the two detectors of the Laser Interferometer Gravitational-Wave Observatory simultaneously observed a transient gravitational-wave signal. The signal sweeps upwards in frequency from 35 to 250 Hz with a peak gravitational-wave strain of 1.0 × 10−21.

papers.title: A Measurement of Excess Antenna Temperature at 4080 Mc/s
papers.abstract: Measurements of the effective zenith noise temperature of the 20-foot horn-reflector antenna at the Crawford Hill Laboratory, Holmdel, New Jersey, at 4080 Mc/s have yielded a value about 3.5° K higher than expected. This excess temperature is, within the limits of our observation, isotropic, unpolarized, and free from seasonal variation.

papers.title: The Large-Scale Structure of the Universe
papers.abstract: This paper discusses the development of the large-scale structure of the universe, including the formation of galaxies, clusters, and superclusters. The analysis presents a comprehensive theory connecting initial conditions to observed cosmic structure.

papers.title: High-Temperature Superconductors
papers.abstract: Possible high-Tc superconductivity in the Ba-La-Cu-O system is observed. Superconductivity was detected above 30 K, a remarkable breakthrough in materials science and condensed matter physics.

papers.title: Quantum Entanglement in Condensed-Matter Systems
papers.abstract: Quantum entanglement in condensed matter systems is reviewed. We discuss the role of entanglement entropy as a diagnostic tool for quantum phases of matter, focusing on spin chains, topological phases, and many-body localization.

papers.title: Black Holes and the Information Paradox
papers.abstract: The formation and evaporation of black holes is discussed in terms of information loss. The information paradox is re-examined in light of quantum mechanics, and a new resolution is proposed involving quantum corrections to the semiclassical geometry.

papers.title: The Cosmological Constant Problem
papers.abstract: Astronomical observations indicate that the universe is expanding at an accelerating rate, implying the existence of a cosmological constant or dark energy. This paper reviews the cosmological constant problem and possible solutions.

papers.title: Topological Quantum Computation
papers.abstract: This paper reviews topological approaches to quantum computation. Topological quantum computers use anyons to perform fault-tolerant quantum calculations, with the topological nature providing inherent protection against decoherence.
papers.journal: Bulletin of the American Mathematical Society

papers.title: Evidence for an Accelerating Universe
papers.abstract: We present spectroscopic and photometric observations of Type Ia supernovae. We confirm the results of Perlmutter et al. suggesting that the universe is accelerating. The data favor a cosmological constant, suggesting that the mass density of the universe is about 0.2 of the critical density.

papers.title: Quantum Computing with Neutral Atoms
papers.abstract: We discuss the prospects for quantum computing using neutral atoms. Recent experimental advances have demonstrated entanglement of multiple atoms and the execution of quantum gates with high fidelity using Rydberg atom interactions.

papers.title: A Programmable Dual-RNA–Guided DNA Endonuclease in Adaptive Bacterial Immunity
papers.abstract: We show that the Cas9 endonuclease can be programmed with guide RNA to cleave specific DNA sites, providing a new tool for genome editing. This breakthrough enables precise modification of DNA sequences in any organism.

papers.title: Hallmarks of Cancer: The Next Generation
papers.abstract: Cancer hallmarks encompass the functional capabilities acquired during tumor development. This review extends the original hallmarks to include two emerging hallmarks—deregulating cellular energetics and avoiding immune destruction—and two enabling characteristics.

papers.title: Gut Microbiota in Human Metabolic Health and Disease
papers.abstract: The gut microbiota is a complex community of microorganisms that plays a crucial role in human health. Here, we review the connections between gut microbiota composition and metabolic diseases, including obesity, type 2 diabetes, and cardiovascular disease.

papers.title: Evolution of Drug Resistance in Malaria Parasites
papers.abstract: Resistance to antimalarial drugs is a major obstacle in the control of malaria. We review the molecular mechanisms of resistance to key antimalarial drugs, the genetic basis of resistance, and strategies to overcome or prevent resistance.

papers.title: Single-Cell RNA Sequencing Technologies and Bioinformatics Pipelines
papers.abstract: Single-cell RNA sequencing (scRNA-seq) has become a powerful tool for examining the transcriptomics of individual cells. We review the major technologies, computational analysis frameworks, and applications of scRNA-seq in biology and medicine.

papers.title: Biodiversity Hotspots for Conservation Priorities
papers.abstract: We identify 25 hotspots for conservation priorities that feature exceptional concentrations of endemic species and are experiencing exceptional loss of habitat. These hotspots contain 44% of all plant species and 35% of all species in four vertebrate groups.

papers.title: The Origin of Species
papers.abstract: On the Origin of Species presents Darwin groundbreaking theory of evolution by natural selection. Darwin argues that species evolve over generations through a process of natural selection, where individuals with favorable traits are more likely to reproduce.

papers.title: Protein Structure Prediction Using Multiple Sequence Alignments
papers.abstract: We present AlphaFold2, a neural network system that accurately predicts protein 3D structure from its amino acid sequence. The system achieves accuracy competitive with experimental methods across most of the protein universe.

papers.title: MicroRNAs: Small RNAs with a Big Role in Gene Regulation
papers.abstract: MicroRNAs (miRNAs) are small non-coding RNA molecules that regulate gene expression in plants and animals. We review the discovery and function of miRNAs and their role in development, cell differentiation, and disease.

papers.title: Global Deforestation and Forest Degradation
papers.abstract: Tropical deforestation and forest degradation are major drivers of climate change, biodiversity loss, and local livelihoods. We review the causes, extent, and consequences of deforestation and discuss policy options to protect forests.
papers.journal: Annual Review of Environment and Resources

papers.title: Global Warming of 1.5°C: IPCC Special Report
papers.abstract: This report examines the impacts of global warming of 1.5°C above pre-industrial levels and related global greenhouse gas emission pathways. It assesses the possibility of limiting warming to 1.5°C and the implications of doing so.

papers.title: The Oceans as a Sink for Atmospheric CO2
papers.abstract: The oceans absorb a significant fraction of the carbon dioxide released by human activities. This study quantifies the uptake of anthropogenic CO2 by the global ocean using a survey of dissolved inorganic carbon.

papers.title: Renewable Energy and Climate Change
papers.abstract: This paper examines the potential contribution of renewable energy to climate change mitigation. It assesses the current state of renewable energy technologies and their potential deployment pathways to achieve climate targets.

papers.title: Planetary Boundaries: Guiding Human Development on a Changing Planet
papers.abstract: The planetary boundaries framework defines a safe operating space for humanity. We present an updated analysis of the nine boundaries and find that four of them—climate change, loss of biosphere integrity, land-system change, and altered biogeochemical flows—have been crossed.

papers.title: Ocean Plastic Pollution: A Brief Review
papers.abstract: Plastic waste input from land into the ocean is estimated at 4.8 to 12.7 million metric tons per year. We review the sources, distribution, and impacts of marine plastic pollution and discuss policy solutions.

papers.title: Deforestation and Climate Change in the Amazon
papers.abstract: The Amazon forest plays a critical role in regulating South American climate and the global carbon cycle. We analyze tipping points for Amazon dieback and discuss the consequences of continued deforestation for regional and global climate.
papers.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences

papers.title: Air Quality and Climate Change
papers.abstract: Air quality and climate change are intrinsically linked through common drivers and interactions. This review examines the co-benefits of policies targeting both climate change and air quality improvement.

papers.title: Sea Level Rise: Current and Future
papers.abstract: We present projections of sea level rise under different greenhouse gas emission scenarios. The results highlight the importance of near-term emission reductions for avoiding extreme sea level rise later this century.
papers.journal: Annual Review of Environment and Resources

papers.title: The Biodiversity Crisis: Losing What Counts
papers.abstract: Despite international conservation commitments, biodiversity continues to decline. This analysis shows that indicators of the status of biodiversity, the pressures upon it, and the responses of the conservation community are mostly moving in the wrong direction.

papers.title: Freshwater and its Management
papers.abstract: Freshwater is essential for human health, food production, and ecosystem function. We review the global availability of freshwater resources, current and projected demand, and the challenges of managing freshwater in a changing climate.
papers.journal: Annual Review of Environment and Resources

papers.title: The Default Mode Network and Self-Referential Processes in Depression
papers.abstract: The default mode network (DMN) is a brain network that is active when individuals engage in self-referential processing. We review evidence that abnormal DMN activity is linked to depression and discuss implications for treatment.

papers.title: Neuroplasticity: Changes in Grey Matter Induced by Training
papers.abstract: We provide in vivo evidence for training-induced plasticity in the adult human brain. Grey matter in subjects trained to juggle showed transient and selective expansion in regions of the brain associated with the processing and storage of complex visual motion.

papers.title: The Neural Correlates of Consciousness
papers.abstract: What are the neural correlates of consciousness? We review current theories and experimental evidence for the neural basis of conscious experience, with a focus on the role of the cortex and thalamocortical interactions.

papers.title: Dopamine and Reward: The Anhedonia Hypothesis
papers.abstract: Dopamine is widely regarded as a critical mediator of reward and motivation. This review examines the role of dopamine in reward, addiction, and psychiatric conditions such as depression and schizophrenia.

papers.title: Alzheimer's Disease: The Amyloid Hypothesis
papers.abstract: The amyloid hypothesis proposes that the accumulation of amyloid-beta peptides in the brain is the primary cause of Alzheimer's disease. We review the evidence for and against this hypothesis and discuss implications for therapy.

papers.title: Income Inequality and Economic Growth
papers.abstract: This paper analyzes the relationship between income inequality and economic growth. We find that inequality tends to be associated with weaker growth, but the effect depends on the type and persistence of inequality.

papers.title: Trade Wars are Class Wars: How Rising Inequality Distorts the Global Economy
papers.abstract: We argue that the root causes of trade imbalances and financial crises lie in the distribution of income within countries. Rising inequality depresses domestic demand and leads to the buildup of excess savings that fuel global imbalances.

papers.title: The Rise and Fall of American Growth
papers.abstract: The American standard of living grew more in the 100 years after 1870 than in any period before or since. We analyze the factors that drove this exceptional growth and argue that future growth will be slower due to structural headwinds.

papers.title: Proof of the Riemann Hypothesis: A New Approach
papers.abstract: We present a new approach to the Riemann Hypothesis using the positivity of certain sums. We define a sequence of positive numbers and show that the Riemann Hypothesis is equivalent to the positivity of this sequence.

papers.title: The Unreasonable Effectiveness of Mathematics in the Natural Sciences
papers.abstract: The enormous usefulness of mathematics in the natural sciences is something bordering on the mysterious. In this paper, we discuss why mathematics is so effective in describing physical phenomena, a question that remains one of the deepest in philosophy of science.
papers.journal: Communications on Pure and Applied Mathematics

papers.title: Carbon Nanotubes: Properties and Applications
papers.abstract: Carbon nanotubes possess exceptional mechanical, thermal, and electronic properties. This review covers the synthesis, functionalization, and application of single-walled and multi-walled carbon nanotubes in electronics, composites, and biomedical devices.

papers.title: Metal-Organic Frameworks: Versatile Materials for Gas Storage
papers.abstract: Metal-organic frameworks (MOFs) are porous crystalline materials with exceptional surface areas and tunable pore sizes. We review their synthesis, properties, and applications in gas storage, separation, and catalysis.

papers.title: Perovskite Solar Cells: Progress and Prospects
papers.abstract: Perovskite solar cells have emerged as a highly promising photovoltaic technology with power conversion efficiencies exceeding 25%. We review the materials science, device physics, and stability challenges of perovskite solar cells.

papers.title: Graphene: Status and Prospects
papers.abstract: Graphene is a single layer of carbon atoms with remarkable electronic properties. This review summarizes the fundamental properties of graphene and discusses its potential applications in electronics, sensors, and composites.

papers.title: Deep Learning for Drug Discovery
papers.abstract: We present a method for molecular property prediction using neural message passing. The model learns from graph representations of molecules and achieves state-of-the-art performance on multiple drug discovery benchmarks.

papers.title: Autonomous Vehicles: Opportunities, Strategies and Disruptions
papers.abstract: This paper reviews the potential impacts of autonomous vehicles on transportation systems, energy use, and urban form. We discuss opportunities for improving safety, mobility, and environmental performance through vehicle automation.

papers.title: 5G Networks: A Survey
papers.abstract: Fifth-generation (5G) wireless networks will transform mobile communications with higher data rates, lower latency, and massive connectivity. This survey covers the key technologies, architectures, and challenges of 5G networks.

papers.title: Additive Manufacturing: A Review of Available Materials
papers.abstract: Additive manufacturing (AM), also known as 3D printing, enables complex geometries to be produced layer by layer from digital models. We review the available materials and processes for AM and discuss applications in aerospace, biomedical, and automotive industries.

papers.title: Smart Grids and the Internet of Things
papers.abstract: Smart grid technologies combined with the Internet of Things (IoT) are transforming energy management. We review architectures for smart energy systems, demand response, and integration of renewable energy sources.

papers.title: Large Language Models in Software Engineering
papers.abstract: We study the application of large language models to software engineering tasks including code completion, bug detection, and program synthesis. Our results demonstrate significant improvements over previous approaches across multiple benchmarks.

papers.title: Recurrent Neural Network Regularization
papers.abstract: We present a simple regularization technique for recurrent neural networks (RNNs) and LSTMs. We show how dropout can be applied to RNNs and achieve state-of-the-art results on language modeling, speech recognition, and machine translation.

papers.title: Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization
papers.abstract: We introduce Adam, an algorithm for first-order gradient-based optimization of stochastic objective functions. The method is straightforward to implement, is computationally efficient, has little memory requirements, and is well suited for problems with large datasets and/or many parameters.

papers.title: U-Net: Convolutional Networks for Biomedical Image Segmentation
papers.abstract: We present a network and training strategy that relies on data augmentation to use the available annotated samples more efficiently. The architecture consists of a contracting path and an expansive path, giving it a U-shaped architecture.

papers.title: Batch Normalization: Accelerating Deep Network Training
papers.abstract: Training Deep Neural Networks is complicated by the fact that the distribution of each layer's inputs changes during training, as the parameters of the previous layers change. We refer to this phenomenon as internal covariate shift, and address the problem by normalizing layer inputs.

papers.title: Proximal Policy Optimization Algorithms
papers.abstract: We propose a new family of policy gradient methods for reinforcement learning, which alternate between sampling data through interaction with the environment, and optimizing a surrogate objective function using stochastic gradient ascent.

papers.title: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
papers.abstract: We study empirical scaling laws for language model performance on the cross-entropy loss. The loss scales as a power-law with model size, dataset size, and the amount of compute used for training, with some trends spanning more than seven orders of magnitude.

papers.title: Random Forests
papers.abstract: Random forests are a combination of tree predictors such that each tree depends on the values of a random vector sampled independently and with the same distribution for all trees in the forest. This paper describes random forests and proves that they achieve low generalization error.

papers.title: Support-Vector Networks
papers.abstract: In this paper we present a new learning machine for two-group classification problems. The training algorithm automatically selects support vectors that are critical for classification, and the classifier is constructed as a linear combination of them.

papers.title: Graph Neural Networks: A Review of Methods and Applications
papers.abstract: Graph neural networks (GNNs) are connectionist models that capture the dependence of graphs via message passing between the nodes of graphs. We review GNN methods and applications in biology, chemistry, social networks, and recommendation systems.

papers.title: Federated Learning: Challenges, Methods, and Future Directions
papers.abstract: Federated learning involves training machine learning models across multiple devices or servers while keeping data local. We survey the key challenges including communication efficiency, systems heterogeneity, statistical heterogeneity, and privacy concerns.

papers.title: Efficacy and Safety of mRNA-1273 SARS-CoV-2 Vaccine
papers.abstract: The mRNA-1273 vaccine showed 94.1% efficacy at preventing Covid-19 illness, including severe disease. The side effects profile was similar to other vaccines with short-lived reactogenicity.

papers.title: The Microbiome in Human Health and Disease
papers.abstract: The human gut contains trillions of microorganisms that have co-evolved with us. This review summarizes the current understanding of the role of the human microbiome in health and disease, including obesity, inflammatory bowel disease, and mental health.

papers.title: Immunotherapy of Cancer with Checkpoint Blockade
papers.abstract: Immune checkpoint blockade antibodies that release brakes on antitumor T-cell immunity have fundamentally transformed cancer therapy. We discuss the mechanism of action, clinical efficacy, and adverse events of checkpoint blockade in multiple tumor types.

papers.title: Gene Therapy Clinical Trials Worldwide
papers.abstract: We review the current state of gene therapy clinical trials worldwide. Significant progress has been made in treating genetic diseases, cancers, and infectious diseases using viral vectors and other gene delivery systems.

papers.title: Precision Medicine in Oncology
papers.abstract: Precision medicine uses genetic, genomic, and other molecular information to tailor medical treatment to individual patients. We discuss the opportunities and challenges of applying precision medicine approaches in oncology.

papers.title: Obesity: Global Epidemiology and Pathogenesis
papers.abstract: Obesity has reached epidemic proportions globally, affecting more than 650 million adults. We review the global epidemiology of obesity, its metabolic and cardiovascular consequences, and the complex pathogenesis involving genetic, behavioral, and environmental factors.

papers.title: Antibiotic Resistance: The Need for Global Solutions
papers.abstract: Antibiotic resistance is a growing threat to global health, food security, and development. This paper identifies key drivers of resistance and proposes a comprehensive global strategy for antimicrobial stewardship.

papers.title: Quantum Computing: An Overview
papers.abstract: Quantum computers exploit quantum mechanical phenomena to solve certain computational problems exponentially faster than classical computers. We review the basic principles of quantum computing, key algorithms, and the current state of hardware development.

papers.title: Dark Matter: A Primer
papers.abstract: We review the evidence for dark matter from multiple independent observations and discuss the leading theoretical candidates. Despite decades of searching, the nature of dark matter remains one of the most important unsolved problems in physics.

papers.title: The Higgs Boson Discovery
papers.abstract: We report the observation of a new boson in the search for the Standard Model Higgs boson with the CMS and ATLAS detectors at the LHC. The new boson has a mass of about 125-126 GeV and properties consistent with the Standard Model Higgs boson.

papers.title: Topological Insulators and Superconductors
papers.abstract: Topological insulators are a new class of quantum matter with a bulk band gap and protected conducting surface states. We review the theoretical predictions and experimental discoveries of topological insulators and superconductors.

papers.title: First Image of a Black Hole: Event Horizon Telescope Results
papers.abstract: We present the first ever image of a black hole obtained by the Event Horizon Telescope. The image reveals a bright ring formed by photons orbiting the massive object, consistent with expectations from general relativity for a black hole of 6.5 billion solar masses.

papers.title: The Human Cell Atlas Project
papers.abstract: The Human Cell Atlas (HCA) is a global initiative to map all cells of the human body. We describe the goals, current progress, and future directions of this ambitious project to create a comprehensive reference map of human cell types.

papers.title: Epigenetics: A Primer
papers.abstract: Epigenetics refers to heritable changes in gene expression that do not involve changes in the DNA sequence. We review the mechanisms of epigenetic regulation, including DNA methylation, histone modification, and chromatin remodeling.

papers.title: The Tree of Life: Evolution of All Organisms
papers.abstract: We reconstruct the tree of life using a large multigene dataset. The results provide insights into the deep evolutionary relationships among all living organisms and support a three-domain classification.
papers.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences

papers.title: Synthetic Biology: Engineering Life
papers.abstract: Synthetic biology applies engineering principles to design biological systems. This paper reviews advances in synthetic gene circuits, metabolic engineering, and the potential applications of synthetic biology in medicine, energy, and materials.

papers.title: Stem Cell Pluripotency and Differentiation
papers.abstract: Induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSCs) can be generated from adult cells by introducing specific transcription factors. This technology has revolutionized regenerative medicine and our understanding of cellular differentiation.

papers.title: The Connectome: A Comprehensive Structural Description of the Brain
papers.abstract: The connectome is the complete map of neural connections in the nervous system. We discuss methods for mapping connectomes, progress in human connectomics, and the relationship between connectivity and function.

papers.title: Sleep and Memory Consolidation
papers.abstract: Sleep plays a critical role in the consolidation of memories. We review the evidence for sleep-dependent memory consolidation and discuss the mechanisms by which different sleep stages contribute to the stabilization of declarative and non-declarative memories.

papers.title: Neuroimaging of Human Cognition
papers.abstract: Neuroimaging techniques such as fMRI and PET have transformed our ability to study human brain function. We review advances in neuroimaging methodology and the insights they have provided into the neural basis of attention, memory, language, and executive function.

papers.title: Predictive Coding as a Theory of Perception
papers.abstract: Predictive coding is a theory of brain function in which the brain constantly generates and updates a mental model of the environment. We review the mathematical formulation of predictive coding and its application to perception, action, and learning.
papers.journal: Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society B

papers.title: Capital in the Twenty-First Century
papers.abstract: This book analyzes a unique collection of data from twenty countries, ranging as far back as the eighteenth century, to uncover key economic and social patterns. Piketty argues that the rate of capital return exceeds the rate of economic growth over the long term, leading to concentration of wealth.

papers.title: Nudge: Improving Decisions About Health, Wealth, and Happiness
papers.abstract: We propose libertarian paternalism as an approach to policy that preserves freedom of choice while nudging people toward decisions that are in their best interest. We discuss applications in retirement savings, health care, and environmental policy.

papers.title: The Great Recession: A Macroeconomic Earthquake
papers.abstract: We analyze the causes, propagation, and aftermath of the 2007-2009 financial crisis and recession. The crisis had multiple causes including the housing bubble, excessive leverage, and regulatory failures, and resulted in the largest economic contraction since the Great Depression.

papers.title: Digital Currencies and the Future of Money
papers.abstract: Central bank digital currencies (CBDCs) and private cryptocurrencies are reshaping the monetary landscape. We analyze the implications of digital currencies for monetary policy, financial stability, and payment systems.

papers.title: The P vs NP Problem
papers.abstract: The P vs NP problem is one of the most important open problems in mathematics and computer science. We review the problem, its history, and the significance of resolving it for cryptography, optimization, and algorithm design.

papers.title: Fermat Last Theorem: The Proof
papers.abstract: We present the proof of Fermat Last Theorem, which states that no three positive integers a, b, c satisfy the equation a^n + b^n = c^n for n > 2. The proof uses modular forms and the theory of elliptic curves.

papers.title: Information Theory and Statistics
papers.abstract: This book presents the application of information theory to statistical problems. We develop the concept of mutual information and Kullback-Leibler divergence and apply them to hypothesis testing, estimation, and coding.

papers.title: Machine Learning: A Probabilistic Perspective
papers.abstract: This textbook covers a broad array of topics in machine learning from a probabilistic perspective. Topics include Bayesian inference, Gaussian processes, deep learning, and graphical models.

papers.title: Stochastic Processes and Their Applications
papers.abstract: We introduce Itô calculus as a tool for analyzing stochastic differential equations driven by Brownian motion. The theory has applications in mathematical finance, physics, and biology.

papers.title: Green Chemistry: Theory and Practice
papers.abstract: Green chemistry aims to design chemicals and processes that reduce or eliminate the use and generation of hazardous substances. We present the twelve principles of green chemistry and discuss their application in pharmaceutical synthesis and industrial chemistry.

papers.title: Organic Solar Cells: An Overview
papers.abstract: Organic solar cells are thin-film photovoltaic devices based on organic semiconductors. We review the operating principles, materials, and performance of organic solar cells and discuss strategies for improving efficiency and stability.

papers.title: Catalysis for Energy: Fundamental Science and Long-Term Impact
papers.abstract: Catalysis is central to the production of fuels and chemicals. We discuss the fundamental principles of heterogeneous catalysis and their application to sustainable energy conversion, including hydrogen production, CO2 reduction, and biomass conversion.

papers.title: Structural Health Monitoring: A Machine Learning Perspective
papers.abstract: Machine learning methods have emerged as powerful tools for structural health monitoring (SHM). We review the application of neural networks, Gaussian processes, and anomaly detection to damage identification in aerospace, civil, and mechanical structures.

papers.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Robotic Manipulation
papers.abstract: We present a deep reinforcement learning approach for robotic manipulation that learns directly from raw image inputs. The system can learn complex manipulation skills such as grasping, placing, and insertion without task-specific reward engineering.

papers.title: Cyber-Physical Systems: A Perspective at the Centennial
papers.abstract: Cyber-physical systems (CPS) are engineered systems that are built from, and depend upon, the seamless integration of computation and physical components. We discuss the unique challenges of CPS design and opportunities for innovation.

papers.title: Wind Energy Technology and Policy: A Survey
papers.abstract: Wind energy has become one of the fastest-growing electricity sources worldwide. We review advances in wind turbine technology, grid integration, and the policies that have driven wind energy deployment.

papers.title: Quantum Cryptography: Public Key Distribution and Coin Tossing
papers.abstract: We present a new cryptographic scheme that is secure against eavesdropping due to the laws of quantum mechanics. The protocol uses quantum channels to establish a shared secret key between two parties.

papers.title: The PageRank Citation Ranking: Bringing Order to the Web
papers.abstract: We describe PageRank, a method for rating Web pages objectively and mechanically, effectively measuring the human interest and attention devoted to them. PageRank relies on the uniquely democratic nature of the web by using its vast link structure as an indicator of an individual page's value.

papers.title: Natural Language Processing with Deep Learning
papers.abstract: This tutorial covers the application of deep learning to natural language processing tasks. Topics include word embeddings, recurrent networks, attention mechanisms, and transformer models for NLP.

papers.title: Efficient Estimation of Word Representations in Vector Space
papers.abstract: We propose two novel model architectures for computing continuous vector representations of words from very large data sets. Our models are described as Word2Vec and achieve significant improvements on word similarity tasks with much lower computational complexity.

papers.title: Variational Autoencoders
papers.abstract: We introduce a new type of generative model called the Variational Autoencoder (VAE). VAEs can be trained efficiently using stochastic backpropagation and can be used for generating new samples from a learned data distribution.

papers.title: Object Detection with Deep Convolutional Neural Networks
papers.abstract: We propose a simple and scalable detection algorithm that improves mean average precision by more than 53% relative to the previous best result on VOC 2012. Our approach combines region proposals with convolutional neural network features.

papers.title: Convolutional Neural Networks for Sentence Classification
papers.abstract: We report on a series of experiments with convolutional neural networks trained on top of pre-trained word vectors for sentence-level classification tasks. We show that a simple CNN with little hyperparameter tuning performs remarkably well.

papers.title: Neural Architecture Search with Reinforcement Learning
papers.abstract: We use a recurrent network to generate model descriptions of neural networks and train this RNN with reinforcement learning to maximize the expected accuracy of the generated architectures on a validation set.

papers.title: YOLO: Real-Time Object Detection
papers.abstract: We present YOLO, a new approach to object detection that reframes object detection as a single regression problem from image pixels to bounding box coordinates and class probabilities. YOLO processes images in real-time at 45 frames per second.

papers.title: DALL-E: Zero-Shot Text-to-Image Generation
papers.abstract: We present a transformer that autoregressively models text and image tokens as a single stream of data. DALL-E can generate diverse and high-quality images from textual descriptions in a zero-shot manner.

papers.title: Whisper: Robust Speech Recognition via Large-Scale Weak Supervision
papers.abstract: We study the capabilities of speech processing systems trained simply to predict large amounts of transcripts of audio. We find that a weakly supervised training approach with 680,000 hours of multilingual data results in a robust speech recognition system.

papers.title: Segment Anything
papers.abstract: We introduce the Segment Anything Model (SAM), a foundation model for image segmentation trained on over 1 billion masks. SAM can segment any object in any image with a simple prompt.

papers.title: AlphaCode: Competition-Level Code Generation
papers.abstract: We introduce AlphaCode, a system that can solve competitive programming problems at a level comparable to human programmers. The system uses a transformer trained on large amounts of code and natural language.

papers.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks
papers.abstract: We explore a general-purpose fine-tuning recipe for retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), which combines parametric and non-parametric memory for language generation. RAG outperforms state-of-the-art models on multiple knowledge-intensive NLP tasks.

papers.title: Constitutional AI: Harmlessness from AI Feedback
papers.abstract: We introduce Constitutional AI, a method for training AI systems to be helpful, harmless, and honest using only AI feedback. The approach uses a set of principles to guide self-critique and revision.

papers.title: Mixtral of Experts
papers.abstract: We introduce Mixtral 8x7B, a sparse mixture of experts language model. Mixtral outperforms Llama 2 70B and GPT-3.5 on most benchmarks while using only a fraction of the inference FLOPs.

papers.title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models
papers.abstract: We explore how chain-of-thought prompting—providing a few examples of chains of reasoning—can improve the reasoning capabilities of large language models. Chain-of-thought prompting enables models to decompose problems into intermediate steps.

papers.title: mRNA Therapeutics: The Road Ahead
papers.abstract: mRNA therapeutics represent a new class of drugs that use the body's own machinery to produce therapeutic proteins. We review the development of mRNA technology, its clinical applications in vaccines and protein replacement therapy.

papers.title: Gut-Brain Axis: How the Gut Microbiome Influences Mental Health
papers.abstract: The gut-brain axis is a bidirectional communication system connecting the enteric nervous system with the central nervous system. We review evidence that the gut microbiome influences brain function, behavior, and mental health.

papers.title: Machine Learning for Medical Diagnosis: Current Applications
papers.abstract: Machine learning algorithms have demonstrated performance matching or exceeding human physicians on several diagnostic tasks including chest X-ray interpretation, skin cancer classification, and diabetic retinopathy screening.

papers.title: Telemedicine and Digital Health: Lessons from COVID-19
papers.abstract: The COVID-19 pandemic dramatically accelerated the adoption of telemedicine. We review the rapid expansion of telehealth services, lessons learned, and the long-term implications for healthcare delivery.

papers.title: The Global Burden of Dementia
papers.abstract: Dementia affects more than 55 million people worldwide and is one of the leading causes of disability in older adults. We review the global epidemiology, risk factors, and economic impact of dementia.

papers.title: CRISPR Therapeutics: Clinical Progress
papers.abstract: CRISPR-based therapies are advancing into clinical trials for genetic diseases, cancers, and infectious diseases. We review the current state of CRISPR therapeutics, key challenges, and the path to clinical approval.

papers.title: String Theory and M-Theory
papers.abstract: String theory proposes that the fundamental constituents of matter are tiny vibrating strings of energy. We review the development of string theory and M-theory, their mathematical structure, and their potential as a unified theory of physics.

papers.title: Quantum Chromodynamics: The Theory of the Strong Force
papers.abstract: Quantum chromodynamics (QCD) is the theory of the strong nuclear force. We review the key features of QCD including asymptotic freedom, color confinement, and the role of gluons in mediating interactions between quarks.

papers.title: Exoplanet Detection by Radial Velocity and Transit Methods
papers.abstract: The detection of exoplanets using radial velocity measurements and the transit method has revealed thousands of planetary systems. We review the techniques for detecting and characterizing exoplanets and discuss the prospects for finding habitable worlds.

papers.title: The Measurement Problem in Quantum Mechanics
papers.abstract: The measurement problem in quantum mechanics concerns the apparent collapse of the wavefunction upon observation. We review the various interpretations of quantum mechanics and their implications for the measurement problem.

papers.title: Neutron Stars and the Equation of State of Dense Matter
papers.abstract: Neutron stars are formed in supernova explosions and represent some of the densest matter in the universe. We review the properties of neutron stars, including their equation of state, magnetic fields, and observations as pulsars.

papers.title: The Great Pacific Garbage Patch: A Review
papers.abstract: We review the distribution, composition, and impacts of plastic debris in the Great Pacific Garbage Patch. Microplastics are particularly problematic, entering the food chain and persisting in the marine environment for centuries.

papers.title: Arctic Sea Ice Decline and Its Global Consequences
papers.abstract: Arctic sea ice extent has declined significantly over recent decades. We analyze the causes and consequences of sea ice loss, including effects on atmospheric circulation, ocean circulation, and the global climate system.

papers.title: Carbon Capture and Storage: Status and Future Prospects
papers.abstract: Carbon capture and storage (CCS) involves capturing CO2 at emission sources and storing it underground. We review the current status of CCS technologies, their costs, risks, and the role they may play in achieving climate targets.
papers.journal: Annual Review of Environment and Resources

papers.title: Ecosystem Services: From Theory to Practice
papers.abstract: Ecosystem services are the benefits people obtain from ecosystems. We review the concept of ecosystem services, methods for their valuation, and the challenges of integrating ecosystem service values into decision-making.

papers.title: The Carbon Budget: Limiting Global Warming
papers.abstract: We analyze the remaining carbon budget consistent with limiting global warming to 1.5°C and 2°C. Our results indicate that rapid and deep decarbonization of the global economy is required to stay within these limits.

papers.title: Telomeres and Aging
papers.abstract: Telomeres are repetitive DNA sequences at the ends of chromosomes that protect them from deterioration. We review the discovery of telomerase, the enzyme that maintains telomere length, and the role of telomere shortening in aging and cancer.

papers.title: The Gut Microbiome in Obesity and Metabolic Disease
papers.abstract: The gut microbiota contributes to the harvest of energy from the diet and to fat storage in the host. We review evidence that the microbiome is altered in obesity and metabolic disease and discuss potential therapeutic implications.

papers.title: Next-Generation Sequencing: Advances and Applications
papers.abstract: Next-generation sequencing (NGS) technologies have revolutionized genomics research. We review advances in NGS platforms, bioinformatics tools for data analysis, and applications in human genetics, cancer genomics, and microbiology.
papers.journal: Annual Review of Genomics and Human Genetics

papers.title: Marine Biodiversity: Current Understanding and Future Research Directions
papers.abstract: Marine biodiversity encompasses the diversity of life in the world's oceans, from microscopic bacteria to great whales. We review current knowledge of marine biodiversity, the threats it faces, and priorities for future research and conservation.

papers.title: Optogenetics: Controlling Neurons with Light
papers.abstract: Optogenetics uses light to control specific neurons genetically modified to express light-sensitive proteins. This review describes the development of optogenetics, key tools, and applications in understanding brain circuits and treating neurological conditions.

papers.title: Deep Brain Stimulation for Neurological Disorders
papers.abstract: Deep brain stimulation (DBS) is an established treatment for movement disorders such as Parkinson's disease. We review the mechanisms, clinical outcomes, and expanding applications of DBS in psychiatry and other neurological conditions.

papers.title: The Brain's Reward System and Addiction
papers.abstract: Addiction is a chronic brain disease characterized by compulsive drug seeking and use. We review the neuroscience of addiction, focusing on the role of the dopamine system and its interactions with the prefrontal cortex.

papers.title: Autism Spectrum Disorder: Genetics and Neurobiology
papers.abstract: Autism spectrum disorder (ASD) is a complex neurodevelopmental disorder with strong genetic contributions. We review the genetics of ASD, the neural circuits involved, and emerging therapeutic targets.

papers.title: The Embodied Mind: Cognitive Science and Human Experience
papers.abstract: We propose an approach to cognitive science that takes seriously both the biological embodiment of the mind and the phenomenological experience of consciousness. Embodied cognition challenges traditional computer metaphors of the mind.

papers.title: The Great Transformation: The Political and Economic Origins of Our Time
papers.abstract: Polanyi argues that the emergence of market society in the nineteenth century was not a natural process but a deliberate political construction. The self-regulating market created social dislocations that eventually provoked political reactions and the collapse of liberal civilization.

papers.title: Efficiency Wages and the Insider-Outsider Theory
papers.abstract: Efficiency wage models propose that firms may find it profitable to pay wages above the market-clearing level to boost worker productivity. We review the efficiency wage hypothesis and related insider-outsider models of unemployment.

papers.title: Behavioral Finance: Investor Psychology and Stock Returns
papers.abstract: We survey the behavioral finance literature, focusing on the psychological biases that affect investor decision-making. We discuss how these biases can lead to predictable patterns in stock prices that deviate from fundamental values.
papers.journal: Handbook of the Economics of Finance

papers.title: Universal Basic Income: A Review of Evidence
papers.abstract: Universal basic income (UBI) has gained prominence as a policy proposal to address poverty and inequality. We review existing evidence on UBI programs, including experiments in Finland, Kenya, and the Alaska Permanent Fund.

papers.title: Cryptocurrency and Blockchain Technology
papers.abstract: Blockchain technology enables decentralized, trustless transactions without intermediaries. We review the principles of blockchain technology, the economics of cryptocurrencies, and potential applications in finance, supply chain, and governance.

papers.title: The Traveling Salesman Problem: An Overview
papers.abstract: The traveling salesman problem (TSP) asks for the shortest route visiting each of a set of cities exactly once. We review exact and heuristic algorithms for the TSP and discuss its relationship to other combinatorial optimization problems.

papers.title: Compressed Sensing: From Theory to Applications
papers.abstract: Compressed sensing is a signal acquisition paradigm that enables accurate reconstruction of sparse signals from far fewer measurements than traditional approaches. We review the theory of compressed sensing and its applications in imaging, communications, and data acquisition.

papers.title: Game Theory and Economic Modelling
papers.abstract: Game theory provides a mathematical framework for analyzing strategic interactions among rational agents. We review the foundations of game theory, including Nash equilibria, evolutionary game theory, and mechanism design.

papers.title: The ABC Conjecture: Progress and Perspectives
papers.abstract: The ABC conjecture is a central conjecture in number theory relating the prime factors of three integers satisfying a + b = c. We review progress toward its proof and connections to other important results in number theory.
papers.journal: Notices of the American Mathematical Society

papers.title: The Chemistry of Natural Products: Terpenes and Alkaloids
papers.abstract: Natural products are organic compounds produced by living organisms. We review the biosynthesis and pharmacological properties of terpenes, alkaloids, and polyketides, and discuss their applications in medicine and agriculture.

papers.title: Lithium-Ion Batteries: Current State and Future Perspectives
papers.abstract: Lithium-ion batteries power portable electronics and electric vehicles. We review the materials science of Li-ion batteries, including cathodes, anodes, and electrolytes, and discuss strategies for improving energy density, safety, and cycle life.

papers.title: Supramolecular Chemistry: From Molecules to Materials
papers.abstract: Supramolecular chemistry studies assemblies of molecules held together by non-covalent interactions. We review the principles of molecular recognition, self-assembly, and the design of supramolecular architectures with functional properties.

papers.title: Click Chemistry for Drug Discovery
papers.abstract: Click chemistry refers to a set of highly reliable and selective chemical reactions for rapid synthesis of new compounds. We describe the development of click chemistry and its applications in drug discovery, chemical biology, and materials science.

papers.title: Neural Network Control Systems
papers.abstract: Neural networks can be used as universal function approximators in control systems. We review the application of neural networks to adaptive control, system identification, and fault-tolerant control of complex dynamical systems.

papers.title: MEMS Technology for Biomedical Applications
papers.abstract: Microelectromechanical systems (MEMS) technology enables miniaturized sensors and actuators for biomedical applications. We review MEMS devices for drug delivery, implantable sensors, lab-on-a-chip systems, and neural interfaces.

papers.title: Photovoltaic Technology: The Case for Thin-Film Solar Cells
papers.abstract: Thin-film solar cells offer potential advantages in cost and material usage compared to crystalline silicon. We review the current status of CdTe, CIGS, and amorphous silicon thin-film technologies.

papers.title: Internet of Things Architecture: A Survey
papers.abstract: The Internet of Things (IoT) refers to the interconnection of everyday objects to the internet, enabling them to send and receive data. We survey IoT architectures, communication protocols, and applications in smart cities, healthcare, and industry.

papers.title: Machine Learning in Power Systems
papers.abstract: Machine learning techniques are being applied to power systems for load forecasting, fault detection, demand response, and optimal dispatch. We review applications of neural networks, reinforcement learning, and Gaussian processes in power system operation.

papers.title: AlexNet: ImageNet Classification with Deep CNNs
papers.abstract: We trained a large, deep convolutional neural network to classify the 1.2 million high-resolution images in the ImageNet LSVRC-2010 contest into the 1000 different classes. The network, which has 60 million parameters and 650,000 neurons, consists of five convolutional layers.

papers.title: Transformer-XL: Attentive Language Models Beyond a Fixed-Length Context
papers.abstract: We propose Transformer-XL, a novel neural architecture that enables learning dependency beyond a fixed length without disrupting temporal coherence. Transformer-XL learns dependency that is 80% longer than RNNs and 450% longer than vanilla Transformers.

papers.title: MobileNet: Efficient Convolutional Neural Networks for Mobile Vision
papers.abstract: We present a class of efficient models called MobileNets for mobile and embedded vision applications. MobileNets are based on a streamlined architecture using depthwise separable convolutions which significantly reduces model size and computation.

papers.title: Stable Diffusion: High-Resolution Image Synthesis with Latent Diffusion Models
papers.abstract: We introduce latent diffusion models (LDMs) which apply diffusion models in a compressed latent space, enabling high-resolution image synthesis with greatly reduced computational requirements. Our model achieves state-of-the-art quality at a fraction of the cost.

papers.title: Mamba: Linear-Time Sequence Modeling with Selective State Spaces
papers.abstract: We introduce Mamba, a new state space model architecture that achieves linear-time inference on sequences. Mamba outperforms Transformers at several tasks while scaling linearly with sequence length.

papers.title: Self-Supervised Learning: The Dark Matter of Intelligence
papers.abstract: Self-supervised learning enables models to learn rich representations from unlabeled data. We review recent advances in self-supervised learning, including contrastive methods, masked autoencoders, and their applications in vision and language.

papers.title: Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks
papers.abstract: Inspired by the Kolmogorov-Arnold representation theorem, we propose Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs) as alternatives to Multi-Layer Perceptrons. KANs have learnable activation functions on edges rather than fixed ones on nodes.

papers.title: Gemini: A Family of Highly Capable Multimodal Models
papers.abstract: We present Gemini, a new family of multimodal models that exhibit remarkable capabilities across image, audio, video, and text understanding. Gemini Ultra achieves human-expert performance on MMLU and other benchmarks.

papers.title: Diabetes and Cardiovascular Risk: Pathophysiology and Treatment
papers.abstract: Cardiovascular disease is the leading cause of death in patients with diabetes. We review the pathophysiology of cardiovascular risk in diabetes, including the roles of hyperglycemia, insulin resistance, and dyslipidemia.

papers.title: Long COVID: Major Findings, Mechanisms and Recommendations
papers.abstract: Long COVID encompasses a wide range of persistent symptoms following acute COVID-19 infection. We review the major findings regarding prevalence, mechanisms, risk factors, and potential treatments for long COVID.

papers.title: GLP-1 Receptor Agonists for Diabetes and Obesity
papers.abstract: Glucagon-like peptide-1 (GLP-1) receptor agonists are a class of medications that lower blood glucose and promote weight loss. We review the mechanisms, clinical efficacy, and safety of GLP-1 receptor agonists for treating type 2 diabetes and obesity.

papers.title: The Lancet Commission on Dementia Prevention
papers.abstract: We identify 12 modifiable risk factors that together account for around 40% of dementia cases. Interventions targeting these risk factors could prevent or delay a large proportion of dementia cases worldwide.

papers.title: Quantum Error Correction: An Introductory Guide
papers.abstract: Quantum error correction is essential for building fault-tolerant quantum computers. We introduce the basic concepts of quantum error correction, including stabilizer codes, the surface code, and threshold theorems.

papers.title: The Physics of Information and the Physics of Computation
papers.abstract: Landauer's principle states that erasing information requires dissipating energy. We review the thermodynamics of computation, Maxwell's demon, and the connections between information theory and physics.

papers.title: Plasma Physics and Thermonuclear Fusion
papers.abstract: Nuclear fusion holds the promise of unlimited clean energy from abundant fuel sources. We review the physics of plasma confinement, the Lawson criterion for achieving net energy gain, and the status of fusion research programs.

papers.title: Synthetic Metagenomics: The Next Wave of Genomic Research
papers.abstract: Metagenomics enables the study of microbial communities directly from environmental samples without culturing. We review advances in metagenomic sequencing, bioinformatics, and the insights they provide into the diversity and function of microbial ecosystems.

papers.title: CRISPR-Based Diagnostics for Infectious Disease
papers.abstract: CRISPR-based diagnostic tools offer rapid, sensitive, and specific detection of pathogens. We review CRISPR-Cas13 and CRISPR-Cas12 diagnostic platforms, including SHERLOCK and DETECTR, and their applications in detecting SARS-CoV-2 and other infectious agents.

papers.title: Organoids: Modeling Human Development and Disease
papers.abstract: Organoids are self-organizing three-dimensional tissue cultures derived from stem cells that recapitulate key features of organs. We review the generation of organoids from human pluripotent stem cells and their applications in disease modeling and drug discovery.

papers.title: Wildfires and Climate: A Two-Way Relationship
papers.abstract: Climate change is increasing the frequency and severity of wildfires worldwide. We review the interactions between wildfires and climate, including feedback effects where wildfires release carbon that further warms the climate.

papers.title: Coral Reef Bleaching and Climate Change
papers.abstract: Global warming is causing mass coral bleaching events with increasing frequency. We document the 2016 and 2017 bleaching events on the Great Barrier Reef and analyze the relationship between ocean temperatures and bleaching severity.

papers.title: The Hydrogen Economy: Opportunities and Challenges
papers.abstract: Hydrogen is being promoted as a clean energy carrier to replace fossil fuels. We review the production, storage, and utilization of hydrogen, and assess the prospects for a hydrogen economy.
papers.journal: Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society A

papers.title: The Trillion Dollar Cure: Psychedelics and Mental Health
papers.abstract: Psychedelic compounds such as psilocybin and MDMA are showing promise in clinical trials for depression, PTSD, and addiction. We review the neuroscience of psychedelics, mechanisms of therapeutic action, and the status of clinical research.

papers.title: Neurofeedback and Brain-Computer Interfaces
papers.abstract: Brain-computer interfaces (BCIs) enable direct communication between the brain and external devices. We review neurofeedback-based BCIs for rehabilitation after stroke, communication in locked-in patients, and enhancement of cognitive performance.

papers.title: Epigenetics and the Brain
papers.abstract: Epigenetic mechanisms including DNA methylation and histone modification play important roles in regulating gene expression in the brain. We review how epigenetic changes contribute to synaptic plasticity, learning, memory, and psychiatric disorders.

papers.title: The Sharing Economy: Economic and Societal Effects
papers.abstract: The sharing economy encompasses peer-to-peer exchanges mediated by digital platforms. We review the economic and societal impacts of sharing economy platforms like Airbnb and Uber, including effects on employment, inequality, and consumer welfare.

papers.title: Automation and the Future of Work
papers.abstract: New technologies are automating tasks previously performed by workers. We analyze the effects of automation on employment and wages, distinguishing between automation that displaces workers and innovation that creates new tasks.

papers.title: Environmental Economics: Pricing Carbon
papers.abstract: We analyze the economics of climate change, proposing an optimal carbon price to internalize the external costs of greenhouse gas emissions. Our DICE model suggests a gradually rising carbon price as the most cost-effective approach to climate policy.

papers.title: Deep Learning Theory: Memorization, Generalization, and Implicit Bias
papers.abstract: We study the theoretical properties of deep neural networks, focusing on their ability to perfectly fit training data while generalizing well to new examples. The double descent phenomenon shows that modern ML models defy classical learning theory.
papers.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences

papers.title: Topology and Geometry in Data Analysis
papers.abstract: Topological data analysis (TDA) uses tools from algebraic topology to study the shape of data. We review persistent homology, mapper, and other TDA methods and their applications in genomics, neuroscience, and materials science.
papers.journal: Bulletin of the American Mathematical Society

papers.title: Optimal Transport: Theory and Applications
papers.abstract: Optimal transport theory studies the most efficient way to move mass from one distribution to another. We review the mathematical foundations of optimal transport and its applications in machine learning, image processing, and economics.

papers.title: Hydrogen Bonding and Protein Folding
papers.abstract: Protein folding is driven by a combination of hydrophobic interactions, hydrogen bonds, and electrostatic forces. We review the principles of protein folding thermodynamics and kinetics and discuss how misfolded proteins lead to disease.

papers.title: Photocatalysis for Solar Fuel Generation
papers.abstract: Solar energy can be used to drive photocatalytic reactions for the production of hydrogen and other solar fuels. We review semiconductor photocatalysts for water splitting and CO2 reduction and discuss strategies for improving efficiency.

papers.title: Catalytic Asymmetric Synthesis
papers.abstract: Asymmetric catalysis enables the synthesis of enantiomerically pure compounds using chiral catalysts. We review the major classes of asymmetric catalysts, including metal complexes, organocatalysts, and enzymes, and their applications in pharmaceutical synthesis.

papers.title: Soft Robotics: A Bioinspired Evolution in Robotics
papers.abstract: Soft robots are constructed from compliant, deformable materials that enable safer interaction with humans and unstructured environments. We review the design principles, materials, actuation, and sensing of soft robots and discuss applications in healthcare and exploration.

papers.title: VLSI Design for Machine Learning Accelerators
papers.abstract: We present the Google Tensor Processing Unit (TPU), an ASIC designed for machine learning inference. The TPU achieves 15x to 30x higher performance per watt than contemporary GPUs and CPUs.

papers.title: Nanotechnology for Drug Delivery
papers.abstract: Nanotechnology enables the design of drug delivery systems with precise control over pharmacokinetics and biodistribution. We review liposomes, polymeric nanoparticles, dendrimers, and inorganic nanoparticles for drug delivery and cancer therapy.

papers.title: Satellite Internet: LEO Constellation Networks
papers.abstract: Low Earth orbit (LEO) satellite constellations such as Starlink and OneWeb are providing broadband internet access globally. We review the architecture, performance, and regulatory challenges of LEO satellite internet systems.

papers.title: Scaling Language Models: Methods, Analysis, and Insights
papers.abstract: We investigate optimal compute allocation for training language models. We find that given a fixed compute budget, the optimal model size and number of training tokens are roughly equal. Chinchilla outperforms GPT-3 and other large models while using less compute.

papers.title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models
papers.abstract: We propose Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA), an efficient method for fine-tuning large language models. LoRA reduces the number of trainable parameters by 10,000x compared to full fine-tuning while achieving comparable or better performance.

papers.title: Instruction Tuning Enables Zero-Shot Task Generalization
papers.abstract: We explore instruction tuning, fine-tuning language models on collections of tasks described via instructions. Instruction-tuned models show strong zero-shot performance on unseen tasks, outperforming few-shot prompted larger models.

papers.title: Reward is Enough
papers.abstract: We argue that the concept of reward is sufficient to drive intelligent behavior. We hypothesize that intelligence, and its associated abilities, can be understood as subserving the maximization of reward in a general reinforcement learning framework.

papers.title: Foundation Models: Opportunities and Risks
papers.abstract: Foundation models are large AI models trained on broad data that can be adapted to a wide range of downstream tasks. We analyze the opportunities and risks of foundation models across language, vision, robotics, and other modalities.

papers.title: Neural Radiance Fields for Novel View Synthesis
papers.abstract: We present Neural Radiance Fields (NeRF), a method for synthesizing novel views of complex scenes by optimizing an underlying continuous volumetric scene function. NeRF represents scenes with implicit neural representations learned from 2D images.

papers.title: OpenAI Codex: Evaluating Large Language Models Trained on Code
papers.abstract: We introduce Codex, a language model fine-tuned on GitHub code. Codex can synthesize programs from docstrings, and we evaluate it on HumanEval, a new benchmark of programming problems. The model solves 28.8% of these problems.

papers.title: TabNet: Attentive Interpretable Tabular Learning
papers.abstract: We propose TabNet, a neural network architecture for tabular learning that uses sequential attention to select features at each decision step. TabNet achieves state-of-the-art performance on tabular benchmarks while remaining interpretable.

papers.title: PaLM: Scaling Language Modeling with Pathways
papers.abstract: We present PaLM, a 540-billion parameter autoregressive language model trained on a curated dataset of 780 billion tokens. PaLM achieves state-of-the-art few-shot performance across hundreds of language understanding and generation benchmarks.

papers.title: The Ethics of Artificial Intelligence
papers.abstract: We discuss the ethical challenges posed by increasingly capable artificial intelligence systems, including issues of bias, fairness, transparency, and the long-term risks of superintelligent AI. We propose principles for the responsible development of AI.

papers.title: Privacy-Preserving Machine Learning: Threats and Solutions
papers.abstract: Machine learning systems may leak sensitive information about training data. We survey privacy-preserving machine learning techniques including differential privacy, federated learning, and secure multiparty computation.

papers.title: Global Health Security After COVID-19
papers.abstract: The COVID-19 pandemic exposed critical gaps in global health security systems. We propose reforms to pandemic preparedness including investment in disease surveillance, international coordination, and equitable vaccine distribution.

papers.title: Aging Hallmarks: What We Know and What We Do Not
papers.abstract: We organize the cellular and molecular hallmarks of aging into three categories: primary, antagonistic, and integrative hallmarks. Understanding these hallmarks is key to developing interventions that extend healthspan.

papers.title: Wearable Technology for Health Monitoring
papers.abstract: Wearable biosensors enable continuous, non-invasive monitoring of physiological parameters. We review advances in electrochemical, optical, and mechanical wearable sensors for monitoring glucose, lactate, cortisol, and other biomarkers.

papers.title: Loop Quantum Gravity: An Introduction
papers.abstract: Loop quantum gravity (LQG) is an attempt to merge quantum mechanics and general relativity. We present the basic ideas and formalism of LQG, including spin networks, spin foams, and the discreteness of space at the Planck scale.

papers.title: Multiverse Theories in Cosmology
papers.abstract: We survey various proposals for a multiverse and classify them into four levels. We argue that accepting the multiverse is a scientific stance, not a philosophical one, and discuss observational tests.

papers.title: RNA Interference: Biology, Mechanism, and Applications
papers.abstract: RNA interference (RNAi) is a mechanism by which double-stranded RNA triggers the degradation of complementary mRNA sequences. We describe the discovery of RNAi and its applications in gene silencing, therapeutics, and functional genomics.

papers.title: Antibiotic Discovery in the Age of CRISPR
papers.abstract: The discovery of new antibiotics has slowed dramatically in recent decades. We review how CRISPR screens, machine learning, and synthetic biology are enabling the discovery of novel antibiotics to address the growing crisis of antimicrobial resistance.

papers.title: Urban Sustainability: Pathways to Net Zero Cities
papers.abstract: Cities are responsible for approximately 70% of global CO2 emissions. We review strategies for decarbonizing cities, including building energy efficiency, renewable energy, sustainable transport, and green infrastructure.
papers.journal: Annual Review of Environment and Resources

papers.title: Deep Sea Ecosystems and Their Vulnerability to Climate Change
papers.abstract: Deep-sea ecosystems support extraordinary biodiversity and provide important ecosystem services. We review the diversity, ecology, and vulnerability of deep-sea communities to climate change, deep-sea mining, and pollution.
papers.journal: Frontiers in Ecology and the Environment

papers.title: Neurogenesis in the Adult Brain
papers.abstract: The adult mammalian brain can generate new neurons throughout life in specific regions including the hippocampus. We review the discovery and regulation of adult neurogenesis and its roles in learning, memory, and mood.

papers.title: Heterogeneous Catalysis: Fundamentals and Applications
papers.abstract: Heterogeneous catalysts accelerate chemical reactions while remaining in a different phase from the reactants. We review the principles of surface science, active site structure, and reaction mechanisms in heterogeneous catalysis.

papers.title: Biomimetic Materials: From Nature to Engineering
papers.abstract: Biological materials such as bone, wood, and silk have evolved remarkable combinations of mechanical properties through hierarchical structuring. We review the design principles of biological materials and their translation into biomimetic engineering.

papers.title: Econometrics of Causal Inference
papers.abstract: Causal inference methods enable researchers to identify causal effects from observational data. We review instrumental variables, regression discontinuity, difference-in-differences, and other methods for causal inference in economics.
